think.notes: Reused epic-level G1 threat model (already covers dogfood activation, signed by ctony 2026-07-20T02:35:00+08:00) -- no fresh G1 needed, no new unknowns found. Two open items carried forward to Plan: pl-scope-guard.mjs allowlist mechanism, activation task-tracking shape.

plan.notes: Change proposed via /spectra-propose, both Think-deferred open items resolved in design.md Decisions (scope-guard allowlist: extend with allowedPaths param, not bypass; activation tracked as this full Spectra change with BDD scenarios, not a manual checklist). spectra analyze: Coverage/Consistency/Gaps clean after 1 fix iteration (5 false-positive capability-coverage findings from backticked path mentions inside Capabilities prose, 4 missing-task findings from requirement names wrapped across markdown line breaks); 3 Suggestion-severity findings remain (missing concrete examples on 3 scenarios) per workflow rule to not block on Suggestions. spectra validate: pass. Change parked -- /spectra-apply not run this session per spectra-propose workflow contract (workflow ends at park, never auto-invokes apply).

build.notes: All 9 tasks done. 1.1-3.2: pl-scope-guard.mjs extended with allowedPaths param (regression + new tests pass); pl-dogfood-activate.mjs (snapshot, atomic apply, manifest) with 4 fixture tests; pl-dogfood-rollback.mjs (restore + verification) with 2 fixture tests. 4.1/4.2 (real-tree verification, paused for human decision on real target-list scope -- chose 'adapter descriptors only'): defined src/scripts/pl-dogfood-target-list.json (single target .claude/commands/pl-check.md, Claude Code adapter only -- no .codex/ dir exists in this repo), ran activation for real against agenticToolset's own tree on branch codex/pldd-dogfood-activation, verified git status showed exactly that one path added (harness live-picked-up the new pl-check command), ran rollback, verified git status/diff empty. 5.1: host-adapters.md updated. Full suite: 364/364 pass, type-check clean. Committed 3ea0b5e (mechanism) + follow-up commit (real-tree proof artifacts + docs), README.md stray edit excluded both times.

review.notes: Adversarial subagent found 6 findings (1 HIGH, 2 MEDIUM, 3 LOW) in pl-dogfood-activate.mjs/pl-dogfood-rollback.mjs/pl-scope-guard.mjs. HIGH fixed: partial apply-phase failure produced no manifest, breaking the rollback recovery contract -- manifest now built incrementally with a partial-manifest attached to thrown errors. MEDIUM fixed: new_hash was computed but never verified (rollback now aborts on pre-restore divergence instead of silently overwriting an intervening edit); allowedPaths entries were unvalidated (now rejects '.', '..', '../'-prefixed entries). 3 LOW findings (no symlink/realpath check, non-unique tmp filenames, TOCTOU) accepted with rationale matching design.md's single-operator/manual-invocation threat model, same class as slice 1's accepted findings. 367/367 tests pass after fixes (3 new), type-check clean, real-tree round-trip re-verified against agenticToolset's own tree with the fixed scripts (still exactly one path changed, still empty diff after rollback).

test.notes: 394/394 tests pass, type-check clean, src-only scope guard clean on every implementation file changed by this slice, npm audit --omit=dev: 0 vulnerabilities. Mutation testing (Stryker, .agents/ts-deliver-router/artifacts/EPIC-PLDD-CONSUMER-ADAPTERS/test-slice2/): first run scored 53.42% -- same class of gap as slice 1 (CLI/isMainModule/runCli paths only reachable via subprocess, uninstrumented by Stryker's in-process runner). Fixed by adding in-process runCli/isMainModule/top-level-CLI-block tests (mirroring pl-scope-guard.test.ts's dynamic-import cache-busting pattern) plus targeted tests for the Review-phase security fixes (unsafe-allowedPaths-entry rejection per entry type, backslash-normalization in allowedPaths, post-restore-verify's independent mismatch detection, partial-manifest-on-failure). Re-ran: 88.03% >= 60% threshold (also clears the 85% high bar) -- not pursued further given threshold met, same stopping point as slice 1. All 7 spec scenarios in specs/pl-dogfood-activation/spec.md map to a passing test. spectra validate: still pass.

G1.notes: Carried forward from epic-level G1 (covers both slices per g1-threat-model.md title and Flow 2 STRIDE analysis). Re-validated this session against slice 1's actual Review/Test findings -- nothing surfaced that the existing threat model failed to anticipate.

G2.notes: Mutation score 88.03% >= 60% threshold (clears 85% high bar too). 1 HIGH + 2 MEDIUM real bugs found and fixed during Review (partial-apply-produces-no-manifest, unverified new_hash allowing silent overwrite of tampered targets, unvalidated allowedPaths entries). Rollback path verified twice: fixture round-trip tests, and a real activate->verify->rollback->verify-empty-diff round-trip against agenticToolset's own live tree (both before and after the Review fixes). 394/394 tests pass, 0 dependency vulns, src-only scope guard clean on every implementation file this slice touched. This is the epic's first-ever live write outside src/* -- human approved per never_automate.md item 3 (non-negotiable, no autonomy-level auto-sign).
